aliases: Steamed Glutinous Rice with Palm Sugar Coconut

english: Steamed Glutinous Rice with Palm Sugar Coconut

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Pulut Inti; mapped to glutinous rice food product as the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Pulut Inti; A0CKP (rice-based snack) used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this traditional Malay kuih; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Pulut Inti is approximately 80 grams, equivalent to one pyramid-shaped piece wrapped in banana leaf.

nutrient_source.title: Singapore Food Composition Database — Kuih and Traditional Snacks; HPB Singapore Foodo
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Pulut Inti values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database for traditional Malay kuih. Input record values (data_source: hpb) are consistent with HPB published ranges for glutinous rice-based kuih with coconut and palm sugar topping. Values retained and cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for glutinous rice (USDA #20052) and sweetened coconut products to confirm plausibility. Saturated fat estimated at 8.5g per 100g based on coconut-dominant fat profile; monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat set to -1 as detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB source for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for glutinous rice
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for Pulut Inti specifically. GI of 75 (high) is estimated based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for glutinous rice (GI ~86–98 range, mean ~92 for plain glutinous rice) and adjusted downward to ~75 to account for the coconut fat and fibre content of the inti topping, which moderates glycemic response. This is consistent with published GI values for glutinous rice-based kuih in the Diabetes Care 2008 international GI tables. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (75 × 36.6g) / 100 = 27.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content at 45 mg per 100g
hypertension.guidance: Pulut Inti is naturally low in sodium (45 mg/100g), making it generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension when consumed in standard single-piece portions; however, total caloric and carbohydrate load should still be monitored.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~75) and high carbohydrate content (45.8g/100g) with 12.5g sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pulut Inti has a high glycemic index (~75) driven by glutinous rice starch and palm sugar, causing rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit portion size to no more than half a piece and pair with low-GI foods to blunt glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat content (~8.5g/100g) from coconut-based inti topping
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The coconut-derived saturated fat in Pulut Inti (~8.5g/100g) may contribute to LDL cholesterol elevation with regular consumption; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake to occasional servings and prioritise overall dietary saturated fat targets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pulut Inti is a traditional Malay kuih (bite-sized snack or cake) consisting of steamed glutinous rice, often tinted blue-purple using natural blue pea flower (bunga telang) extract, topped with a sweet, fragrant mixture of freshly grated coconut cooked with palm sugar (gula melaka). The dish is traditionally shaped into a small pyramid and wrapped in a fresh banana leaf, which imparts a subtle herbal aroma to the rice. The texture is characteristically sticky and chewy from the glutinous rice, contrasted by the moist, caramelised sweetness of the coconut topping. Pulut Inti is a staple of Malay breakfast culture and afternoon tea-time (minum petang) across Malaysia and Singapore, commonly found at traditional kuih stalls and morning markets (pasar pagi). Nutritionally, it provides 285 kcal per 100g, with carbohydrates as the dominant macronutrient (45.8g/100g), moderate fat from coconut (10.2g/100g, predominantly saturated), and modest protein (3.5g/100g). It has a high glycemic index (~75) due to glutinous rice starch and added palm sugar, making it unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is plant-based, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is best enjoyed as an occasional snack rather than a daily staple for those monitoring blood glucose or cardiovascular risk.

confidence_reason: Input record is sourced from HPB Singapore (canonical provenance, confidence 0.95). Core macros are consistent with HPB published ranges for glutinous rice-based kuih. Saturated fat was estimated from coconut fat profile as HPB source does not publish full fatty acid breakdown for this item. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for glutinous rice, adjusted for coconut fat moderating effect. Overall confidence is medium due to the saturated fat estimation and analogous GI assignment rather than direct measurement.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown and moisture data for Pulut Inti are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026. Saturated fat (8.5g/100g) was estimated from the known fatty acid profile of fresh grated coconut (USDA FoodData Central #12117) scaled to the coconut content of the inti topping. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg are reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible/trace): this is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients; vitamin B12 and vitamin D are naturally absent; vitamin A is negligible as confirmed by HPB source data. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero as this is a fully plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients. 4. GI value of 75 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database glutinous rice entries; no direct GI measurement for Pulut Inti was found in approved sources. 5. Glycemic load per serving = (75 × 36.64g available carbohydrate per 80g serving) / 100 = 27.5 (rounded). 6. Multilingual names for Tamil and Hindi are transliterations of the Malay name as no distinct Tamil or Hindi culinary names exist for this dish in the Singaporean context.
